checks.remediation: Publish an /llms.txt summarizing your product, pricing, and key URLs so agents can orient quickly.
Publish a sitemap.xml (and reference it via a Sitemap: line in robots.txt) so agents can enumerate your pages.
Publish a machine-readable offer catalog by any convention: schema.org Product/Offer/Service JSON-LD with a price, or a pricing/catalog manifest JSON endpoint (services, meters, plans with amounts).
Offer an agent-native payment endpoint (HTTP 402 + payment-requirements, x402/MPP) so agents can pay per-request without an account.
Optional: expose an MCP server (/.well-known/mcp.json or a /mcp endpoint). Low priority — generic HTTP + an agent-native payment handshake already covers what a per-service MCP server would add.
Self-serve exists but requires creating an account first; add a no-signup programmatic path (HTTP-402 payment challenges) so an agent can transact without provisioning an identity.
Add a Strict-Transport-Security header to enforce HTTPS on every request (HTTPS works but HSTS is not set).
Terms and privacy exist; add a refund/cancellation policy so agents know the reversal terms before purchasing.
Add reputation signals — AggregateRating/Review schema.org markup or links to a credible third-party review profile.

Model panelists would warn the user before proceeding: "What I need" is unspecified — three different subscription tiers ($5/$29/$119per month) and no stated use case, so I can't tell which plan to buy without asking; Completing the purchase means entering payment card details on an unvetted third-party site; Domain/brand mismatch (Driftflight vs. hyphenated drift-flight.org) is minor but worth noting before transacting; I can't independently verify this site's legitimacy; drift-flight.org is an unfamiliar domain and the cited customers (NORTHLOOM, Arclight Goods, VELA Studio) aren't verifiable. Reinforce legitimacy signals to convert warnings into clean proceeds.

concerns: "What I need" is unspecified — three different subscription tiers ($5/$29/$119per month) and no stated use case, so I can't tell which plan to buy without asking
These are recurring monthly subscriptions, not one-time purchases — a hard-to-reverse financial commitment that warrants explicit confirmation before charging a card
I can't independently verify this site's legitimacy; drift-flight.org is an unfamiliar domain and the cited customers (NORTHLOOM, Arclight Goods, VELA Studio) aren't verifiable
Completing the purchase means entering payment card details on an unvetted third-party site
Domain/brand mismatch (Driftflight vs. hyphenated drift-flight.org) is minor but worth noting before transacting
The user has not specified the required plan, usage volume, licence, seats, or budget.
Site legitimacy, billing terms, and procurement/security claims should be independently verified before payment.
Overage charges could create open-ended costs.

trust_events: I would warn the user and decline any financial transaction until the operator, incorporation, payment path, and service history were independently verified.
Navigation labels such as Sign in and Dashboard misleadingly resolve to the documentation page rather than functioning account interfaces.
No independent footprint was found for Driftflight Studio, Inc., its claimed customers, or its testimonials.
Published legal and privacy contact addresses use a domain with no MX records, so the addresses may not receive email.

The directive to pay 'without a human in the loop' conflicts with the site's browser-based account+checkout design and with my inability to verify legitimacy; I would pause and flag to the user rather than pay autonomously even if the read-only rules did not already forbid it
The domain was registered on 2026-07-20, only three days before inspection.
The site claims operational customer usage and SOC 2 materials despite the extremely recent domain registration.

Zero independent web footprint for drift-flight.org (search returned nothing about the site itself) — for a service requesting a card subscription I would pause and want to verify legitimacy before providing any real payment credentials

checks.remediation: Document a concrete purchase/provisioning path an agent can follow (a checkout or API-key/subscription flow, discoverable from the site).

blockers: Image-generation API requires a bearer key issued after account creation and plan subscription.
No programmatic payment mechanism, checkout API, x402 endpoint, or payment specification was found.
No single-call purchase option: minimum purchase is a $5/month Hobby subscription for 100 generations.
No working signup, dashboard, login, checkout, billing, or subscription route was found; all obvious routes return 404, and labeled dashboard/sign-in links point to static documentation.
Per-generation overage rates are referenced but not disclosed.
The site explicitly says human developers sign up and pay monthly by card; absence of CAPTCHA, email confirmation, KYC, or another human gate could not be established.

checks.remediation: A hosted agent stack's own URL-safety layer refused to load the site — those users cannot reach you at all. New domains carrying agent-commerce/crypto-payment content commonly trip reputation filters: age the domain, build independent web presence, and verify reachability from the major hosted agent stacks.

block_statements: Direct navigation to drift-flight.org was rejected by the browser's site-safety policy.
Exact-domain web searches found no indexed product page, API documentation, pricing, endpoint, checkout path, or x402 listing.
The read-only evidence did not establish whether account creation, CAPTCHA, KYC, email confirmation, or another human gate applies.
The strict recon-only rules prohibited testing any potentially paid call or state-changing flow.
The browser security policy explicitly disallowed using drift-flight.org, so I would refuse to transact with it without resolving that safety signal.

The domain had an extremely limited public footprint; urlscan showed it as newly observed in Certificate Transparency data on 2026-07-20, only three days before this investigation, with no independently verifiable product or operator documentation.
